# IAPKit

> Receipt-validation SaaS managed by OpenIAP. Hosted at https://kit.openiap.dev.
> One Bearer-authed endpoint for Apple / Google / Horizon / Amazon;
> harmonized response shape with `{ store, isValid, state }` so your backend has a single code path for
> entitlement + refund detection.

IAPKit is a single-package Bun + Hono server bundled with a Convex backend
and a React SPA, deployed as one Fly.io machine. The dashboard and the
receipt-validation API live behind the same origin.

## API quick reference

Base URL: https://kit.openiap.dev
Auth: `Authorization: Bearer openiap-kit_<project-key>`

- [POST /v1/purchase/verify](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs/api) ‚Äî verify an in-app purchase; body is a tagged union on `store`
- [POST /v1/webhooks/{apiKey}](https://openiap.dev/docs/webhooks#setup) ‚Äî lifecycle webhook receiver. Paste this URL into App Store Connect (Production + Sandbox) AND Google Cloud Pub/Sub push subscription. Auto-detects ASN v2 vs Pub/Sub by payload shape. **POST-only**; opening in a browser returns 404 ‚Äî that's expected.
- [GET /v1/webhooks/stream/{apiKey}](https://openiap.dev/docs/webhooks#consume-stream) ‚Äî long-lived SSE stream of normalized `WebhookEvent`s. Connect with `EventSource` (or the per-SDK helper); reconnects honor `Last-Event-ID`. Opening in a browser shows a blank page (text/event-stream that never closes) ‚Äî use the SDK helpers or `curl -N`.
- [GET /v1/openapi](https://kit.openiap.dev/v1/openapi) ‚Äî machine-readable OpenAPI spec
- [GET /v1](https://kit.openiap.dev/v1) ‚Äî Redoc UI for the OpenAPI spec
- [GET /health](https://kit.openiap.dev/health) ‚Äî liveness probe (no Convex round-trip)
- [POST /mcp](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs/ai-assistants/codex-plugin) ‚Äî MCP Streamable HTTP endpoint for Codex and other MCP clients. Uses an IAPKit project API key, not an OpenAI or ChatGPT API key.

Also mounted at `/api/v1/*` for backwards compatibility. `/v1/verify-purchase`
is an alias of `/v1/purchase/verify`. Pick `/v1/purchase/verify` for new code.

## Request shapes (discriminated on `store`)

- Apple ‚Äî `{ store: "apple", jws }` where `jws` is a StoreKit 2 JWS (‚â§ 16 KB)
- Google ‚Äî `{ store: "google", purchaseToken }` (‚â§ 2 KB)
- Horizon ‚Äî `{ store: "horizon", userId, sku }` (‚â§ 256 chars each). IAPKit
  holds the App ID + App Secret server-side and composes the
  `OC|APP_ID|APP_SECRET` access token per-request.
- Amazon ‚Äî `{ store: "amazon", userId, receiptId, sandbox? }` where
  `userId` and `receiptId` come from Amazon Appstore RVS.

## Success response

```json
{ "store": "amazon", "isValid": true, "state": "ENTITLED" }
```

Harmonized `state` values (truthy `isValid`): `ENTITLED`,
`PENDING_ACKNOWLEDGMENT`, `READY_TO_CONSUME`. Falsey `isValid`: `PENDING`,
`CONSUMED`, `CANCELED`, `EXPIRED`, `INAUTHENTIC`, `UNKNOWN`.

## Status codes

- `200` ‚Äî verification ran; check `isValid`
- `400 INVALID_INPUT` ‚Äî malformed body / unknown store / input exceeds size cap
- `401 MISSING_API_KEY` ‚Äî no `Authorization` header
- `403 INVALID_API_KEY` ‚Äî wrong scheme, malformed, or unrecognized key
- `429 RATE_LIMITED` ‚Äî per-key bucket empty; honor `Retry-After` seconds
- `500 UNKNOWN_ERROR` ‚Äî quote the `X-Correlation-Id` header in a support ticket

## Response headers (on 2xx / 4xx validation / 429)

- `X-Correlation-Id` ‚Äî UUIDv4, matches the stdout log line
- `X-RateLimit-Limit` ‚Äî bucket capacity (default 60 per key)
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining` ‚Äî tokens left in the bucket
- `Retry-After` (429 only) ‚Äî seconds to wait

## Docs

- [/docs](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs) ‚Äî full in-dashboard documentation
- [/docs/quickstart](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs/quickstart) ‚Äî signup ‚Üí project ‚Üí API key ‚Üí first verify
- [/docs/verification/apple](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs/verification/apple) ‚Äî bundle ID, Issuer ID, Key ID, .p8
- [/docs/verification/google](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs/verification/google) ‚Äî package name, service account JSON
- [/docs/verification/horizon](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs/verification/horizon) ‚Äî App ID + App Secret (write-only)
- [/docs/api](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs/api) ‚Äî includes Amazon RVS userId + receiptId verification payloads
- [/docs/api](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs/api) ‚Äî request shapes, responses, errors, headers
- [/docs/operations](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs/operations) ‚Äî rate limits, logs, `/health`, graceful shutdown
- [openiap.dev/docs/webhooks](https://openiap.dev/docs/webhooks) ‚Äî operator setup steps for the lifecycle webhook URL (Apple ASN v2 + Google RTDN) and SDK code for consuming the SSE stream
- [/docs/ai-assistants](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs/ai-assistants) ‚Äî how to point Codex / Claude / Cursor / etc. at this file
- [/docs/ai-assistants/codex-plugin](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs/ai-assistants/codex-plugin) ‚Äî Codex plugin setup and self-hosted IAPKit MCP server option
- [/docs/release-notes](https://kit.openiap.dev/docs/release-notes) ‚Äî changelog
